description: Negative conformance fixtures for signer_record key-source binding. All six vectors represent admission claims that a lenient verifier might silently accept but that fail at least one signer_record invariant.

vectors.description: Two-signer admission row. Primary signer_record is complete (embedded key, correct signature_input_hash). Co-signer record has a valid JWS signature but neither public_key_hash nor key_resolution_evidence_hash is present — the co-signer leg is not recomputable from the fixture alone.
vectors.failure_detail: signer_records[1].public_key_hash absent and key_resolution_evidence_hash absent. Co-signer leg requires out-of-band key fetch — not independently recomputable.

vectors.description: Co-signer key resolved from external JWKS. key_resolution_time is 2026-06-29T08:00:00Z, which is after the fixture certification time (2026-06-28T10:00:00Z). The key record post-dates the certified artifact — the key that was used during original admission cannot be confirmed.
vectors.failure_detail: signer_records[1].key_resolution_time='2026-06-29T08:00:00.000Z' is after fixture certification time '2026-06-28T10:00:00.000Z'. Key cannot be proven to have been the key in use during original admission.

vectors.description: Co-signer public key is present and cryptographic verification passes, but signer_id 'anonymous-key-holder' is not linked to any declared verifier role. The key proves ownership of a key pair, not independent admission authority.
vectors.failure_detail: signer_records[1].signer_id='anonymous-key-holder' is not bound to a declared verifier role. Cryptographic verification passing does not establish admission independence.

signer_records.verification_note: Cryptographic verification passes. signer_id not bound to any declared role.

vectors.description: Board reports admission pass. Co-signer record has key_source absent — the verifier cannot determine if the key came from the fixture, a live fetch, a registry, or a cache. The claim is not recomputable because the resolution path is unknown.
vectors.failure_detail: signer_records[1].key_source is absent. Without key_source, a verifier cannot determine the resolution path and cannot confirm independent recomputability.

vectors.description: Co-signer signature_input_hash is f8dbea22… — SHA-256 of the pretty-printed JSON envelope, not SHA-256(JCS(envelope)) = 906a51a3…. The signer signed different bytes than the canonical envelope. The admission does not bind to the certified artifact.
vectors.failure_detail: signer_records[1].signature_input_hash='f8dbea22…' != canonical_envelope_hash='906a51a3…'. Signer signed pretty-printed JSON bytes, not JCS. Admission is not bound to the canonical certified envelope.

signer_records.verification_note: Signed pretty-printed JSON bytes, not JCS canonical form.

vectors.description: Admission row claims two independent signers. Primary leg is fully recomputable (embedded key, correct signature_input_hash). Co-signer verification_result is 'unverifiable' — key could not be resolved from the fixture. Reporting two-signer recomputation is an overclaim: only one leg is independently recomputable.
vectors.failure_detail: signer_records[1].verification_result='unverifiable'. Only 1 of 2 signer legs is independently recomputable from the fixture. Claiming two-signer admission recomputation is an overclaim.

signer_records.verification_note: JWKS endpoint returned 404 at resolution time. Key not retrievable from fixture or external source.
